commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e1866deb7c59dc5324c0d85cb4e7735b8f243b36)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan` to prefer a raw `modelOverride` string over the resolver output when returning and patching the child model.

PR surface: Source +2. Total +2 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced regression: source and tests show aliases are canonicalized today, while the patch stores the raw override. No high-confidence live reproduction is provided for the linked `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` report.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the proposed root cause conflicts with current resolver behavior and the branch needs repro-backed direction rather than a mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(agents/subagent-spawn): Sub-agent model routing ignores model parameter
target.body:
Fixes #91171. AI-generated fix, human-reviewed.

## Summary
When `sessions_spawn` is called with an explicit `model` parameter (e.g. `qwen/qwen3.6-plus`), the resolved model was correctly computed but then discarded in `resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan` — the return object used the auto-resolved `resolvedModel` instead of the user-specified `modelOverride`.

## Fix
In `subagent-spawn-plan.ts`, introduce `effectiveModel` which prioritizes `modelOverride` over `resolvedModel`:
```typescript
const effectiveModel = params.modelOverride?.trim() || resolvedModel;
```
This ensures that when a user explicitly specifies a model, that model is used for both the returned `resolvedModel` and the `initialSessionPatch.model`.

## Real behavior proof
- pnpm lint:core passed (baseline errors ignored)
- The fix is a minimal 4-line change that directly addresses the root cause

## What was not tested
Full integration tests in live environment.
target.author: tanshanshan
